replacements: Personalize your feed / subscribe to channels
Verwenden der Social Wall (nicht überall verfügbar)

new_pages.title: Shift Schedules, Swaps & Time Off
new_pages.content:
Your schedule, swaps, and time-off requests

This page walks through how scheduling works at Ross — for store associates and DC associates. If anything here conflicts with what your manager has told you, follow your manager.

Where to see your schedule

Your schedule is in the Schedule app (linked from Ross Connect, pinned to bottom nav on mobile). Schedules are published two weeks in advance. If yours isn't visible 10+ days out, talk to your manager.

Requesting a shift swap

In the Schedule app, tap the shift and choose Request Swap. Offer it to the team or a specific associate. Manager approval needed — usually within 24 hours.

PTO and unpaid time off

Submit through the Time Off portal (linked from Ross Connect). Requests 2 weeks in advance are easier to accommodate. Peak season (Black Friday → New Year's) observes blackout periods.

Calling out

If you can't make a shift, call your store/DC at least 2 hours before your shift starts. Don't rely on text messages.

new_pages.title: Inventory, Receiving & Markdowns
new_pages.content:
How inventory moves at Ross

Off-price is a flow business. Newness lands every week.

Receiving

  1. Confirm carton count vs. manifest.
  2. Stage cartons by department in the backroom.
  3. Process by department; tag, hang, and floor-ready per SOP.
  4. Move to floor — don't let it sit.

Markdowns

  • First markdown — light reduction; less-prime spot.
  • Second (red) — moves to clearance.
  • Third / clearance (yellow) — final before transfer/salvage.

RTV and salvage

Damaged/defective goes into RTV bins. Follow the salvage SOP. Don't put RTV back on the floor.

new_pages.title: Loss Prevention & Shrink
new_pages.content:
LP basics at Ross

Every associate plays a part. Three categories: external theft, internal theft, process loss.

External theft

Best deterrent: great customer service. If you see suspicious behavior — don't confront. Observe, document, notify your manager/LP partner. Your safety first.

Internal theft

Rare but devastating. Report concerns confidentially via the Ethics Hotline.

Process loss

Quiet category — RTV not shipped, salvage paperwork missed. Tight execution closes the gap.

EAS / InVue

High-value categories get tags/locks. Detachers stay at cash wrap. If a tag won't release, call a manager — don't force it.

Incident reporting

Every theft incident — caught or escaped — documented in the LP system within 24 hours.

new_pages.title: Associate Discount & Benefits
new_pages.content:
Associate Discount

All eligible associates receive a 20% in-store discount at Ross Dress for Less.

  • Active associates in good standing.
  • Eligibility starts after initial waiting period (typically 30 days; varies).
  • Personal use only — not for resale.

Show your associate ID at checkout. Shop on your own time. Use your own payment method.

Benefits

Vary by role and hours. Check Benefits portal for what applies to you.

  • Medical, dental, vision (eligible associates).
  • 401(k) with company match.
  • EAP — free, confidential counseling.
  • Paid time off, accrued by hours.
  • Tuition reimbursement (eligible).
  • Adoption assistance and parental leave.

Open enrollment: November. Benefits team hosts Q&A sessions.

new_pages.title: Store Opening & Closing Procedures
new_pages.content:
Opening and closing your store

Owned by manager on duty. Full SOP in the store binder.

Open

  1. Manager arrives 30 min before. Confirms alarm disarmed, walks floor.
  2. Cash room: count tills, verify safe, prepare register starts.
  3. Floor walk: pick up tags, recover departments.
  4. Team huddle (5 min): focus, callouts, CS goal.
  5. Unlock doors at posted time.

Close

  1. Last-customer announcement at posted closing time. Doors lock 1 min after.
  2. Recover the floor by department.
  3. Cash room: pulls, deposit prep, safe drop.
  4. Final walk, lights down, alarm set.
  5. Two associates leave together — never alone.

Cash room

Authorized associates only. Two-person rule for deposits.

new_pages.title: Code of Business Conduct (Quick Reference)
new_pages.content:
How we work — Code of Business Conduct (quick reference)

The full Code is required reading. This page is a short summary; not a replacement.

Our standards

  • Honesty. Customer interactions, cash handling, performance feedback.
  • Respect. Every associate, customer, vendor, community.
  • Compliance. Laws and regulations everywhere we operate.
  • Privacy. Protect customer/associate/vendor information.
  • No retaliation. Speaking up in good faith is protected.

Conflicts of interest

Relationships affecting impartiality → disclose. Disclosure is straightforward; non-disclosure is the problem.

Gifts and entertainment

Modest vendor gifts may be acceptable. Cash/gift cards never. When in doubt, ask.

Reporting

Manager, HR, Ethics Hotline (anonymous), legal. Retaliation = Code violation.
